title: Contact -
text_excerpt: Contact - Skip to main content Join Donate Home About Contact Emergency Help Office Open Hours Help from Peter Work Experience What can’t we help with? News Events My Work Science, Innovation and Technology Future of Work and Employment Domestic Abuse in Family Courts Trains Our NHS Homelessness Patient Transport School Funding BEIS Committee Fighting Climate Change Police Funding Donate More Menu Contact EMERGENCY HELP You can often get immediate help from these contacts; In an emergency always call 999 Police non-emergency service : 101 NHS medical help : 111 or https://111.nhs.uk/ Council Website and Switchboard : https://www.brighton-hove.gov.uk/ or 01273 29 00 00 ACAS (for National Minimum Wage, working hours and employment agency issues): 0300 123 1100 Access Point (Adult social care): 01273 295 555 Age Concern : https://www.ageuk.org.uk/westsussexbrightonhove or 01273 720 603 Report Anti-Social behaviour : https://www.brighton-hove.gov.uk/community-safety-partnership/anti-social-behaviour-asb-and-hate-incidents Crime Stoppers : 0800 555 111 Environmental services (Roads and pavements, waste collections, public toilets, street cleaning and environmental enforcement): 01273 29 29 29 General Money Guidance : https://www.moneyhelper.org.uk/en or 0800 138 7777 General Housing Advice : https://www.brighton-hove.gov.uk/housing Private Housing Advice : 01273 293 156 Council Housing Tenants : 01273 296 622 Noise Patrol : 01273 700812 Parking Permit Services : 01273 296 622 Pensions Guidance : http://moneyhelper.org.uk/en/pensions-and-retirement/pension-problems or 0800 011 3797 Rubbish, Recycling and Streets : https://www.brighton-hove.gov.uk/rubbish-recycling-and-streets Tax Credit Helpline : 0345 300 3900 Winter Fuel Payment Helpline : 0800 731 0160 If you do not have access to a phone, you can visit Customer Service Centres in Brighton (Jubilee Library), or Hove (Hove library) and use phones and computors there to contact council services. HELP FROM YOUR MP Peter has surgeries with constituents on Fridays, usually in the constituency office in Hove. This is run on an appointment system. You can email (please include your name and address) at hove.portslade@parliament.uk Alternatively, our specialist caseworker, Stella Pentecost, can see you Monday – Thursday. >>>> Please email hove.portslade@parliament.uk or call 01273 933380 for help and advice<<<< His constituency office is at: 99 Church Road Hove BN3 2BA TEL: 01273 933380 We can only accept casework from constituents living in Hove, Portslade or Hangleton. If you send emails or letters please provide a confirmation address. We cannot act on behalf of residents in another constituency. You should always make contact with your own Member of Parliament. What issues CAN’T we deal with? If you have a problem which is the responsibility of local government, such as your local council, you should contact the local councillor. If you have tried to seek support without success we may be able to look into the matter for you. You can find your local councillor HERE https://present.brighton-hove.gov.uk/mgFindMember.aspx There are some things my office cannot do, these include: Give legal advice or interfere with ongoing legal proceedings Offer consumer or contract advice Offer debt advice or advice on financial services Become involved in disputes with other individuals Requests to sign or witness official documents Peter can only sign passports or official documents if he knows you personally, in line with the guidance issued with the application form. It isn’t enough to ‘know of you’ and MPs will usually apply a high bar of personal knowledge of the constituent before agreeing to sign official documents. If you want to contact Peter about a policy or political matter you can contact him at; peter.kyle.mp@parliament.uk WORK EXPERIENCE Peter runs a very busy and accessible constituency office in Hove.
